risk_description: The original doctoral database contains sensitive adolescent information.
mitigation: Do not store, copy, describe or publish the original data.

risk_description: Public-use Add Health files still contain individual-level survey records.
mitigation: Keep all microdata local and exclude them through .gitignore.

risk_description: Outcomes related to pregnancy, HIV and sexual risk behaviour require careful handling.
mitigation: Use neutral academic language and publish only aggregate outputs.

risk_description: Restricting to grades 10-12 and possibly ages 15-19 may reduce cell sizes.
mitigation: Review sample size after every restriction and report attrition transparently.
project_rule: Check sample size after grade and age filters

risk_description: Small cells may increase disclosure risk in tables or figures.
mitigation: Suppress or aggregate small cells before publication.

risk_description: Some Add Health files and identifiers require restricted-use contracts.
mitigation: Classify such variables as restricted and do not use without approval.
project_rule: Use public data only unless formal access exists

risk_description: Friend, sibling, romantic partner, school or cluster identifiers may be restricted, masked or unavailable.
mitigation: Do not publish identifiers or outputs that allow re-identification.

risk_description: Accidental upload of raw data would create an ethical and legal problem.
mitigation: Commit only scripts, documentation and reviewed aggregate outputs.
project_rule: Check git status before every commit

risk_description: Poor wording may stigmatise adolescents or imply moral judgement.
mitigation: Use precise epidemiological and econometric language.
